author: John Chrysostom

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “So when the Samaritans were come unto Him, they besought Him that He would tarry with them: and He abode there two days.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And many more believed because of His own Word; and said unto the woman, Now we believe, not because of thy saying: for we have heard Him ourselves, and know that This is indeed the Christ, the Saviour of the world.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Now after two days He departed thence, and went into Galilee.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Nothing is worse than envy and malice, nothing more mischievous than vainglory; it is wont to mar ten thousand good things.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: So the Jews, who excelled the Samaritans in knowledge, and had been always familiar with the Prophets, were shown from this cause inferior to them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For these believed even on the testimony of the woman, and without having seen any sign, came forth beseeching Christ to tarry with them; but the Jews, when they had beheld His wonders, not only did not detain Him among them, but even drove Him away, and used every means to cast Him forth from their land, although His very Coming had been for their sake.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: The Jews expelled Him, but these even entreated Him to tarry with them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Was it not then rather fitting, tell me, that He should receive those who asked and besought Him, than that He should wait upon those who plotted against and repulsed Him, while to those who loved and desired to retain Him He gave not Himself?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Surely this would not have been worthy of His tender care; He therefore both accepted them, and tarried with them two days.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: They desired to keep Him among them continually, (for this the Evangelist has shown by saying, that “they besought Him that He would tarry with them,”) but this He endured not, but stayed with them only two days; and in these many more believed on Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Yet there was no likelihood that these would have believed, since they had seen no sign, and had hostile feelings towards the Jews; but still, inasmuch as they gave in sincerity their judgment on His words, this stood not in their way, but they received a notion which surmounted their hindrances, and vied with each other to reverence Him the more.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For, saith the Evangelist, “they said to the woman, Now we believe, not because of thy saying: for we have heard Him ourselves, and know that this is indeed the Christ, the Saviour of the world.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: The scholars overshot their instructress.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: With good reason might they condemn the Jews, both by their believing on, and their receiving Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: The Jews, for whose sake He had contrived the whole scheme, continually were for stoning Him, but these, when He was not even intending to come to them, drew Him to themselves.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And they, even with signs, remain uncorrected; these, without signs, manifested great faith respecting Him, and glory in this very thing that they believe without them; while the others ceased not asking for signs and tempting Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Such need is there everywhere of an honest soul; and if truth lay hold on such an one, she easily masters it; or if she masters it not, this is owing not to any weakness of truth, but to want of candor in the soul itself.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Since the sun too, when he encounters clear eyes, easily enlightens them; if he enlightens them not, it is the fault of their infirmity, not of his weakness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Hear then what these say; “We know that this is of a truth the Christ, the Saviour of the world.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Seest thou how they at once understood that He should draw the world to Him, that He came to order aright our common salvation, that He intended not to confine His care to the Jews, but to sow His Word everywhere?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: The Jews did not so, but going about to establish their own righteousness, submitted not themselves to the righteousness of God; while these confess that all are deserving of punishment, declaring with the Apostle, that “all have sinned, and come short of the glory of God; being justified freely by His grace.” ( Rom. iii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For by saying that He was “the Saviour of the world,” they showed that it was of a lost world, and He not simply a Saviour, but one of the very mightiest.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For many had come to “save,” both Prophets and Angels ; but this, saith one, is the True Saviour, who affordeth the true salvation, not that which is but for a time.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: This proceeded from pure faith.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And in both ways are they admirable; because they believed, and because they did so without signs, (whom Christ also calleth “blessed,” saying, “Blessed are they that have not seen, and yet have believed,”) ( c. xx.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 29 ,) and because they did so sincerely.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Though they had heard the woman say doubtfully, “Is not this the Christ?” they did not also say, “we too suspect,” or, “we think,” but, “we know,” and not merely, “we know,” but, “we know that this is of a truth the Saviour of the world.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: They acknowledged Christ not as one of the many, but as the “Saviour” indeed.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Yet whom had they seen saved?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: They had but heard His words, and yet they spake as they would have spoken had they beheld many and great marvels.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And why do not the Evangelists tell us these words, and that He discoursed admirably?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: That thou mayest learn that they pass by many important matters, and yet have declared the whole to us by the event.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For He persuaded an entire people and a whole city by His words.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: When His hearers are not persuaded, then the writers are constrained to mention what was said, lest any one from the insensibility of the hearers should give a judgment against Him who addressed them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Now after two days He departed thence and went into Galilee.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “For Jesus Himself testified that a Prophet hath no honor in his own country.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Wherefore is this added?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Because He departed not unto Capernaum, but into Galilee, and thence to Cana.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For that thou mayest not enquire why He tarried not with His own people, but tarried with the Samaritans, the Evangelist puts the cause, saying that they gave no heed unto Him; on this account He went not thither, that their condemnation might not be the greater.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For I suppose that in this place He speaketh of Capernaum as “His country.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Now, to show that there He received no honor, hear Him say, “And thou, Capernaum, which art exalted unto heaven, shalt be brought down to hell.” ( Matt. xi.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He calleth it “His own country,” because there He set forth the Word of the Dispensation, and more especially dwelt upon it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “What then,” saith some one, “do we not see many admired among their kindred?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: In the first place such judgments must not be formed from rare instances; and again, if some have been honored in their own, they would have been much more honored in a strange country, for familiarity is wont to make men easily despised.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Then when He was come into Galilee, the Galilæans received Him, having seen all the things that He did at Jerusalem at the feast, for they also came unto the feast.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Seest thou that these men so ill spoken of are found most to come to Him?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For one said, “Can there any good thing come out of Nazareth?” ( c. i.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 46 ), and another, “Search and look, for out of Galilee ariseth no prophet.” ( c. vii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: These things they said insulting Him, because He was supposed by the many to be of Nazareth, and they also reproached Him with being a Samaritan; “Thou art a Samaritan,” said one, “and hast a devil.” ( c. viii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Yet behold, both Samaritans and Galilæans believe, to the shame of the Jews, and Samaritans are found better than Galilæans, for the first received Him through the words of the woman, the second when they had seen the miracles which He did.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “So Jesus came again into Cana of Galilee, where He made the water wine.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: The Evangelist reminds the hearer of the miracle to exalt the praise of the Samaritans.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: The men of Cana received Him by reason of the miracles which He had done in Jerusalem and in that place; but not so the Samaritans, they received Him through His teaching alone.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: That He came then “to Cana,” the Evangelist has said, but he has not added the cause why He came.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Into Galilee He had come because of the envy of the Jews; but wherefore to Cana?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: At first He came, being invited to a marriage; but wherefore now?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Methinks to confirm by His presence the faith which had been implanted by His miracle, and to draw them to Him the more by coming to them self-invited, by leaving His own country, and by preferring them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “And there was a certain nobleman whose son was sick at Capernaum.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “When he heard that Jesus was come out of Judæa into Galilee, he went unto Him and besought Him that He would come down and heal his son.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: This person certainly was of royal race, or possessed some dignity from his office, to which the title “noble” was attached.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Some indeed think that this is the man mentioned by Matthew ( Matt. viii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 5 ), but he is shown to be a different person, not only from his dignity, but also from his faith.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: That other, even when Christ was willing to go to him, entreats Him to tarry; this one, when He had made no such offer, draws Him to his house.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: The one saith, “I am not worthy that Thou shouldest come under my roof”; but this other even urges Him, saying, “Come down ere my son die.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: In that instance He came down from the mountain, and entered into Capernaum; but here, as He came from Samaria, and went not into Capernaum but into Cana, this person met Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: The servant of the other was possessed by the palsy, this one’s son by a fever.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “And he came and besought Him that He would heal his son: for he was at the point of death.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What saith Christ?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Except ye see signs and wonders, ye will not believe.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Yet the very coming and beseeching Him was a mark of faith.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And besides, after this the Evangelist witnesses to him, declaring that when Jesus said, “Go, thy son liveth,” he believed His word, and went.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What then is that which He saith here?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Either He useth the words as approving of the Samaritans because they believed without signs; or, to touch Capernaum which was thought to be His own city, and of which this person was.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Moreover, another man in Luke, who says, “Lord, I believe,” said besides, “help Thou mine unbelief.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And so if this ruler also believed, yet he believed not entirely or soundly, as is clear from his enquiring “at what hour the fever left him,” since he desired to know whether it did so of its own accord, or at the bidding of Christ.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: When therefore he knew that it was “yesterday at the seventh hour,” then “himself believed and his whole house.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Seest thou that he believed when his servants, not when Christ spake?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Therefore He rebuketh the state of mind with which he had come to Him, and spoken as he did, (thus too He the more drew him on to belief,) because that before the miracle he had not believed strongly.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: That he came and entreated was nothing wonderful, for parents in their great affection are also wont to resort not only to physicians in whom they have confidence, but also to talk with those in whom they have no confidence, desiring to omit nothing whatever.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Indeed, that he came without any strong purpose appears from this, that when Christ was come into Galilee, then he saw Him, whereas if he had firmly believed in Him, he would not, when his child was on the point of death, have hesitated to go into Judæa.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Or if he was afraid, this is not to be endured either.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Observe how the very words show the weakness of the man; when he ought, after Christ had rebuked his state of mind, to have imagined something great concerning Him, even if he did not so before, listen how he drags along the ground.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Sir,” he saith, “come down ere my child die.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: As though He could not raise him after death, as though He knew not what state the child was in.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: It is for this that Christ rebuketh him and toucheth his conscience, to show that His miracles were wrought principally for the sake of the soul.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For here He healeth the father, sick in mind, no less than the son, in order to persuade us to give heed to Him, not by reason of His miracles, but of His teaching.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For miracles are not for the faithful, but for the unbelieving and the grosser sort.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: At that time then, owing to his emotion, the nobleman gave no great heed to the words, or to those only which related to his son, yet he would afterwards recollect what had been said, and draw from thence the greatest advantage.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: As indeed was the case.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But what can be the reason why in the case of the centurion He by a free offer undertook to come, while here though invited, He goeth not?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Because in the former case faith had been perfected, and therefore He undertook to go, that we might learn the rightmindedness of the man; but here the nobleman was imperfect.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: When therefore he continually urged Him, saying, “Come down,” and knew not yet clearly that even when absent He could heal, He showeth that even this was possible unto Him in order that this man might gain from Jesus not going, that knowledge which the centurion had of himself.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And so when He saith,“Except ye see signs and wonders, ye will not believe,” His meaning is, “Ye have not yet the right faith, but still feel towards Me as towards a Prophet.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Therefore to reveal Himself and to show that he ought to have believed even without miracles, He said what He said also to Philip, “Believest thou that the Father is in Me and I in the Father?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Or if not, believe Me for the very works’ sake.” ( c. xiv.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “And as he was now going down, his servants met him, and told him, saying, Thy son liveth.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Then enquired he of them the hour when he began to amend.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And they said unto him, Yesterday at the seventh hour the fever left him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: So the father knew that it was at the same hour in the which Jesus said unto him, Thy son liveth; and himself believed, and his whole house.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Seest thou how evident the miracle was?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Not simply nor in a common way was the child freed from danger, but all at once, so that what took place was seen to be the consequence not of nature, but the working of Christ.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For when he had reached the very gates of death, as his father showed by saying, “Come down ere my child die”; he was all at once freed from the disease.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: A fact which roused the servants also, for they perhaps came to meet their master, not only to bring him the good news, but also deeming that the coming of Jesus was now superfluous, (for they knew that their master was gone there,) and so they met him even in the way.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: The man released from his fear, thenceforth escaped into faith, being desirous to show that what had been done was the result of his journey, and thenceforth he is ambitious of appearing not to have exerted himself to no purpose; so he ascertained all things exactly, and “himself believed and his whole house.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For the evidence was after this unquestionable.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For they who had not been present nor had heard Christ speak nor known the time, when they had heard from their master that such and such was the time, had incontrovertible demonstration of His power.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Wherefore they also believed.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What now are we taught by these things?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Not to wait for miracles, nor to seek pledges of the Power of God.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: I see many persons even now become more pious, when during the sufferings of a child or the sickness of a wife they enjoy any comfort, yet they ought even if they obtain it not, to persist just the same in giving thanks, in glorifying God.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Because it is the part of right-minded servants, and of those who feel such affection and love as they ought for their Master, not only when pardoned, but also when scourged, to run to Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For these also are effects of the tender care of God; “Whom the Lord loveth He chasteneth, and scourgeth,” it says, “every son whom He receiveth.” ( Heb. xii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: When therefore a man serves Him only in the season of ease, he gives proofs of no great love, and loves not Christ purely.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And why speak I of health, or abundant riches, or poverty, or disease?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Shouldest thou hear of the fiery pit or of any other dreadful thing, not even so must thou cease from speaking good of thy Master, but suffer and do all things because of thy love for Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For this is the part of right-minded servants and of an unswerving soul; and he who is disposed after this sort will easily endure the present, and obtain good things to come, and enjoy much confidence in the presence of God; which may it be that we all obtain through the grace and lovingkindness of our Lord Jesus Christ, to whom with the Father and the Holy Ghost be glory, now and ever, and world without end.
